page: Microsoft Azure E96d_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled, offering 3686 GB of capacity. The server runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include any GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 93261.07 and a PassMark CPU score of 76355.96. Cryptographic capabilities are highly efficient, as evidenced by a top-tier PassMark CPU encryption test score of 87642.66, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets. Memory bandwidth is also robust, reaching 5005.88 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 327.92 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1680.0.

While multi-threaded operations, database tasks, and static web serving perform in the top 25% of tested systems, large-scale LLM text generation represents a performance bottleneck, with the 70B model achieving only 2.68 tokens/sec. The high memory density and bundled local storage make this server qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads and database operations that benefit from localized storage, rather than raw single-threaded compute or GPU-accelerated tasks. It is highly suited for relational databases, caching layers like Redis, software compilation, and data-dense enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure E96d_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs across 48 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM and 3686 GB of bundled local storage, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The server demonstrates strong multi-core and memory performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 76355.96 and a top-tier encryption score of 87642.66. Memory read bandwidth reaches 327.92 GB/sec for large blocks, supporting high-throughput operations. However, single-core performance is average, and large LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale database operations, and Redis caching layers that require substantial RAM and local storage without GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96d_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and 3686 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96d_v5 is a memory-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and 3686 GB of bundled local storage.
tagline: A high-capacity memory and storage configuration designed for demanding database operations, multi-threaded workloads, and enterprise caching applications.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 672 GB of RAM optimized for memory-intensive applications.
Bundled 3686 GB of local storage for high-speed data access.
Top-tier cryptographic performance with hardware-accelerated encryption.
Strong multi-core processing and high memory bandwidth.
Best suited for large databases, Redis caching, and software compilation.
